message.reasoning:
We need to respond with JSON only. The task: Gather exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions so fixtures etc stay versioned and aligned.

We have a hint: Selected History shows request to expand file node F1. The tool_call was session.expand_file@v1 with payload status unknown etc. The result status ok but no details given. Probably we need to read more of the file to find schema version constants. The objective: need exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions. Likely need to locate the exact lines defining schema version constants, maybe to check current versions.

We should probably hydrate source or read file content to see definitions like FIXTURE_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION etc. The graph shows file node F1 is crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs. We need to get its content. The tool session.expand_file expands file symbols. It was called with unknown status but result says ok. Maybe need to read the file content.

We can use session.hydrate or session.hydrate_source to get the content? There's also graph.resolve? Probably use session.hydrate to hydrate source for file node.

We need to return JSON with kind maybe tool_call or complete depending.

We need to decide next action: we need to read file content to locate schema version constants. That likely requires using session.hydrate or session.hydrate_source? The tool list includes session.hydrate@v1, session.hydrate_source@v1.

Better to use session.hydrate with target selector maybe "F1" or "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Use selector "F1". Provide payload.

Thus kind: tool_call, tool_name: session.hydrate@v1, payload with target selector "F1". acquired_fact_ids maybe list of fact ids? Probably empty array [].

We need to output JSON with fields: kind, assistant_message, tool_call, acquired_fact_ids, readiness_state.

Readiness_state maybe "not_ready" because we haven't gathered evidence yet.

So output:

{
 "kind":"tool_call",
 "assistant_message":"Hydrating file F1 to locate schema version constants.",
 "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.hydrate@v1","payload":{}},
 "acquired_fact_ids":[],
 "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

But need payload specifics: According to tool contract, session.hydrate payload includes target selector and optional padding, hydration budgets. Use target selector "F1". So payload: {"target":"F1"} maybe.

Thus JSON:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Hydrate file node F1 to view its content and locate schema version constants.",
  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.hydrate@v1","payload":{"target":"F1"}},
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

Make sure no extra fields. Ensure JSON only.
